channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: GitHub Advanced Security now offers security campaigns
item.description: GitHub has made security campaigns available for GitHub Advanced Security and GitHub Code Security users. Security campaigns help control security debt and manage risk by enabling collaboration between developers and security teams, GitHub said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Instagram bans livestreaming for kids without parental consent
item.description: Meta announced on Tuesday new “built-in protections” for its Instagram Teen Accounts, including a new requirement for parents to consent before children under 16 can go “live” on the platform or unblur nudity in images they receive in direct messages.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Former Facebook executive tells Senate committee company undermined US national security with China
item.description: Sarah Wynn-Williams, a former executive at Meta and author of an explosive insider account of her time at the social media giant, testified before the Senate Judiciary Committee Wednesday, accusing the company of undermining national security and briefing China on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Whistleblower tells senators that Meta undermined U.S. security, interests
item.description: Sarah Wynn-Williams, the former director of global public policy at Facebook, which is now called Meta, testified at a Senate hearing Wednesday that she saw Meta executives “repeatedly undermine U.S. national security and betray American values” during her seven-year stint in that job.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Scientists map part of a mouse's brain that's so complex it looks like a galaxy
item.description: Thanks to a mouse watching clips from “The Matrix,” scientists have created the largest functional map of a brain to date.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dan Clark Audio's Noire X are the Johnny Cash of elite planar magnetic headphones and I must have them
item.description: Looking for the ultimate Man in Black cans? You just found them, although the price-tag may still (ahem) Hurt…
item.dc:creator: becky.scarrott@futurenet.com (Becky Scarrott), TechRadar

item.title: Man who used AI lawyer explains why he did so after judge got angry
item.description: Dewald maintains he received prior approval for his presentation method, though not specifically for an AI-generated speaker.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: YouTube entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: YouTube entrepreneur and star MrBeast slammed President Trump’s “brutal” tariffs in a post on the social platform X on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: New observations show the asteroid that won't hit Earth resembles a spinning hockey puck
item.description: The asteroid that once had a small chance of striking Earth and now might slam into the moon resembles a spinning hockey puck.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Democrats demand information from Microsoft, Google on AI deals
item.description: A pair of Senate Democrats are calling on Microsoft and Google to share more information on their separate partnerships with artificial intelligence (AI) companies, expressing concerns the collaborations could violate antitrust laws. In letters sent Monday, Sens. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: IRS acting commissioner is resigning over deal to send immigrants' tax data to ICE, AP sources say
item.description: The Internal Revenue Service’s acting commissioner is resigning over a deal to share immigrants’ tax data with Immigration and Customs Enforcement for the purpose of identifying and deporting people illegally in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta is bringing stricter parental controls to Facebook and Messenger
item.description: Meta is bringing its Teen Accounts, which have stricter parental controls, to its Facebook and Messenger platforms on Tuesday, expanding its teen service from just Instagram.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Unlocking the Power of Bookmarklets with Generative AI
item.description: Discover how generative AI like ChatGPT can help you create custom bookmarklets to streamline your web browsing experience
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Musk calls Navarro 'moron' after 'demonstrably false' claims about Tesla
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk slammed President Trump's trade adviser Peter Navarro on Tuesday, calling him "truly a moron" in their latest spat over the administration's new import taxes. "Navarro is truly a moron.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Anthropic expands in Europe with new roles and EMEA chief
item.description: Anthropic plans to add more than 100 roles across Europe, indicating a broader push to expand its global footprint amid rising demand for enterprise-ready AI tools.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hydrogen fuel cell trucks hit the roads in Georgia
item.description: Hydrogen trucks debut in Georgia, sparking debates on costs and green freight gains. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson comments on this promising chapter in green logistics evolution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Redbox goes up for auction following a wild bankruptcy saga
item.description: Redbox is getting ready for one final sale. The defunct DVD rental chain’s assets, and those of its corporate siblings Crackle and Chicken Soup for the Soul Entertainment, are being auctioned off in New York later this month, according to a court filing published Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke: AI is now a ‘fundamental expectation’ for employees
item.description: Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke shared an internal memo on X Monday that stressed the importance of using AI effectively in daily tasks. In fact, he wrote, using AI is now a “fundamental expectation” of Shopify employees.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Cloudflare unveils agentic AI development tools
item.description: Following on its February release of the Agents SDK JavaScript framework for building AI agents, Cloudflare began its Developer Week today by announcing a series of tools and features designed to accelerate agentic AI development.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: President Trump on Monday threatened to impose a 50 percent tariff on imports from China, a massive escalation of a potential trade war between the world's two largest economies. Trump's threat is the latest tit for tat between the U.S. and China in the last week.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: 9 steps to take to prepare for a quantum future
item.description: Over the past year, vendor after vendor has reached the critical quantum-computing milestone where adding more qubits no longer adds a disproportionately higher amount of errors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 10 Java-based tools and frameworks for generative AI
item.description: Java is not the first language most programmers think of when they start projects involving artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML). Many turn first to Python because of the large number of Python-based frameworks and tools for AI, ML, and data science.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Amanda Nguyen to become first Vietnamese American woman in space
item.description: Nguyen will join Aisha Bowe, Gayle King, Katy Perry, Kerianne Flynn and Lauren Sánchez on an all-female flight.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: 'Battlestar Galactica' is 'even more relevant now,' star says
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How to remotely reset your router: Steps for easy troubleshooting
item.description: You can reset your internet router remotely if you're not home when your power goes out. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how this can be done.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Meta is fined by Turkey after refusing to restrict content on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: Meta said it has been hit with a hefty fine for resisting Turkish government demands to limit content on Facebook and Instagram.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
